skill.name: Azure.AI.DocumentIntelligence (.NET)
skill.description: Azure AI Document Intelligence SDK for .NET. Extract text, tables, and structured data from documents using prebuilt and custom models. Use for invoice processing, receipt extraction, ID document analysis, and custom document models.
skill.summary: Extract text, tables, and structured data from documents using Azure Document Intelligence SDK in .NET
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only documentation skill containing Azure Document Intelligence SDK code examples. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) with 0 security issues detected. The skill is purely a reference guide with no executable code, scripts, or network calls. No suspicious patterns or risk factors identified.

content.user_title: Extract Document Data with Azure AI
content.value_statement: Process invoices, receipts, and documents automatically using Azure Document Intelligence SDK in .NET. Extract structured data, text, and tables from PDFs and images without manual data entry.
actual_capabilities: Analyze invoices and extract vendor, line items, and totals with confidence scores
Extract text, tables, and structure from PDF documents using prebuilt-layout model
Process receipts to capture merchant name, items, and transaction totals
Build custom document models for specialized document types using Azure Blob storage
Classify documents automatically using custom document classifiers
Authenticate using Microsoft Entra ID or API key for Azure services
limitations: Requires Azure Document Intelligence resource with valid endpoint and authentication
Custom model training requires labeled training data in Azure Blob storage
Analysis is asynchronous and may take time for large documents
Accuracy depends on document quality and clarity of scanned content

use_cases.title: Automate Invoice Processing
use_cases.description: Extract vendor names, invoice numbers, line items, and totals from PDF invoices automatically. Reduce manual data entry and processing time.
use_cases.target_user: Finance teams and accounts payable departments

use_cases.title: Receipt Digitization for Expenses
use_cases.description: Capture receipt data including merchant, items purchased, taxes, and totals. Store structured data for expense reporting systems.

use_cases.title: Build Custom Document Extraction
use_cases.description: Train custom models to extract specific fields from company-specific documents like contracts, forms, or custom reports.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Invoice with Azure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Azure.AI.DocumentIntelligence to analyze an invoice from URL. Show me how to extract vendor name, invoice total, and line items with confidence scores in C#.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extract invoice data from a PDF document

prompt_templates.title: Extract Text and Tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use the prebuilt-layout model to extract all text content and tables from a PDF document using Azure Document Intelligence client in .NET.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extract full document structure and tables

prompt_templates.title: Build Custom Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build a custom document model using Azure.AI.DocumentIntelligenceAdministrationClient. I have labeled training data in Azure Blob storage.

prompt_templates.title: Authenticate with Entra ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate to Azure Document Intelligence using DefaultAzureCredential in a .NET application. What endpoint format is required?

output_examples.output:
Vendor Name: 'Acme Corp', confidence: 0.95
Invoice Total: '$1,234.56'
Line Items:
  - Widget A (5 units): $500.00
  - Service Fee: $234.56
  - Tax: $100.00

Table 1: 5 rows x 3 columns
  Cell (0,0): Product | Quantity | Price
  Cell (1,0): Widget A | 5 | $500.00

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential in production for secure Microsoft Entra ID authentication instead of API keys
Always check the Confidence property on extracted fields to verify data quality
Reuse DocumentIntelligenceClient instances as they are thread-safe and designed for repeated use
anti_patterns: Do not create a new client for each document analysis operation - this is inefficient
Avoid using API keys in client-side applications - prefer Entra ID authentication
Do not skip confidence score checking - low confidence values may indicate extraction errors

faq.question: What is the difference between DocumentIntelligenceClient and DocumentIntelligenceAdministrationClient?
faq.answer: DocumentIntelligenceClient is used for analyzing documents and classifying them. DocumentIntelligenceAdministrationClient is used for building and managing custom models and classifiers.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Document Intelligence?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production with Microsoft Entra ID, or AzureKeyCredential with an API key for development. Entra ID requires a custom subdomain endpoint.

faq.question: Can I extract data from scanned images?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Document Intelligence supports JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, and PDF files including scanned documents. The service performs OCR automatically.

faq.question: What prebuilt models are available?
faq.answer: Available prebuilt models include: read, layout, invoice, receipt, idDocument, businessCard, tax.us.w2, and healthInsuranceCard.us.

faq.question: How do I build a custom model?
faq.answer: Use DocumentIntelligenceAdministrationClient with BuildDocumentModelAsync. Provide training documents in Azure Blob storage with SAS URL. You need at least 5 labeled documents.

faq.question: What is the difference between Template and Neural build modes?
faq.answer: Template mode is for fixed-layout documents like forms. Neural mode handles variable-layout documents like invoices and receipts with better accuracy.
